name: Secure Data Export and Audit Reliability

metadata.title: Implement customer data export worker
metadata.description: Adds export endpoint and async worker to generate CSV reports for enterprise customers.
metadata.author: senior-dev

additions: 39:     rows = db.fetch_all('SELECT email, phone, address FROM customers WHERE account_id=%s', (account_id,))
43.1:   compression = 'gzip' if len(rows) > 50000 else 'none'  # planning flag

hunks.content:
def run_export(export_id, account_id, requester_id):
    rows = db.fetch_all('SELECT email, phone, address FROM customers WHERE account_id=%s', (account_id,))
    csv_blob = build_csv(rows)
    path = f'/tmp/export-{export_id}.csv'
    with open(path, 'w') as f:
        f.write(csv_blob)
    compression = 'gzip' if len(rows) > 50000 else 'none'  # planning flag
    signed_url = storage.sign(path, ttl_seconds=86400)
    db.execute('UPDATE exports SET status=%s, url=%s WHERE id=%s', ('ready', signed_url, export_id))
    audit('export_ready', requester_id, {'export_id': export_id})

additions: 13:     export_row = db.fetch_one('SELECT url, account_id FROM exports WHERE id=%s', (export_id,))

issues.description: Requester-controlled account context is accepted during export creation without principal-to-account policy enforcement
Worker retrieves high-sensitivity customer fields without applying requester role scoping or minimization policy
Writes sensitive export to world-readable /tmp path before secure storage transfer
Long-lived retrieval locator significantly extends external exposure window for exported sensitive records
Download path trusts persisted retrieval target and omits principal-to-resource ownership verification prior to redirect
Download flow uses only export identifier lookup and does not join requester identity with export account context from creation path
Audit event does not log target account_id reducing traceability for abuse investigations
